title: How to proceed with electric power steering (EPS) failure diagnosis

blocks.text: How to proceed with electric power steering (EPS) failure diagnosis

The EPS warning light (A) illuminates in the ignition ON mode when the system is normal and turns off when the engine starts. If a system malfunction is detected when the engine starts, the light does not turn off. The EPS warning light also comes on if a malfunction occurs after the engine starts and the EPS warning light goes off. The EPS warning light may remain on until the detected abnormality returns to normal and the ignition is turned to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, or it may automatically turn off as soon as the detected abnormality returns to normal.

When extreme steering maneuvers are repeatedly performed, such as continuous stationary steering, the amount of motor power increases, causing the motor and EPS control unit to generate heat. Since this heat generation adversely affects the system, the motor current is monitored, and if the motor current is overheated, the power assist is limited by gradually decreasing the motor current to protect the system. The power assist limitation is released as soon as the system is out of the heat generated by the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode or by decreasing the steering wheel turning torque.
Normally, the system returns to the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, but in case of excessive heat generation, the engine is restarted after a maximum of approximately 18 minutes.

If noise is generated in conjunction with steering wheel operation while listening to the AM radio, the ground line of the motor may be defective. If such symptoms occur, check for a broken ground line, faulty connection terminal, or faulty ground (G401).

items: The HDS (Honda Diagnostics System) must be connected and disconnected with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode.

blocks.text: 1. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the driver's side with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode. 2. Turn ignition to ON mode. 3. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the EPS control unit. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry. 4. Check the DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code) displayed on the HDS.

items.text: For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.
HDS can display various EPS control unit data, such as freeze data and snapshots, and can be used for diagnostic purposes by selecting the necessary mode.

items: HDS must be connected and disconnected with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode.

blocks.text: 1. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the driver's side with the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode. 2. Turn ignition to ON mode. 3. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the EPS control unit. If it does not communicate, Inspect the data link circuitry. 4. Use HDS to clear DTC. For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual.
